title: Implement POST /api/recipes (create)
description:
Part of parent issue BE-006: Build recipes router (CRUD endpoints)

This unit only: Implement POST /api/recipes (create)

Add the POST handler at backend/app/api/routes/recipes.py. The router file does not yet exist; create it.

The handler signature must be:

```python
@router.post('', response_model=RecipeOut, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_recipe_endpoint(
    payload: RecipeCreate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    user = Depends(require_roles(['user', 'admin'])),
) -> RecipeOut:
    ...
```

Key rules:
- owner_id is derived ONLY from the JWT (via ensure_local_user); never from payload
- require_roles dependency handles 401 (no/invalid token) and 403 (missing user/admin role)
- RecipeCreate's extra='forbid' rejects client-supplied owner_id/id with 422 automatically
- Match async style to the surrounding skeleton conventions (check auth/*.py)
- The router declares `prefix='/recipes'` (the auto-mount adds /api/v1)
- Module docstring + helpful comments are expected

Helpers to call:
- `app.services.recipes.create_recipe(session, owner_id=..., data=payload)`
- `app.services.recipes.ensure_local_user(session, jwt_claims=claims)`
success_criteria: app/api/routes/recipes.py exists with a POST '' handler
Handler derives owner_id from JWT, not payload
Handler calls app.services.recipes.create_recipe with the owner_id and payload
